methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Luma AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Runway
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Magic AI, a Series C stage AI & Software company founded by Eric Steinberger and Sebastian De Ro, is evaluated against a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $1.5B on $466M total raised.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement to the $1.5B base, implying $2.5B. Peer-Median analysis references 4 same-sector, same-stage comps (Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Luma AI at $4.0B, and Runway at $1.5B), yielding a median valuation of $9.0B with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous market assessments of AI software value capture.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.8B. This range reflects material uncertainty around the company's ability to sustain competitive positioning and revenue scale relative to the broader peer set. Key sensitivities include the normative relevance of extreme peer valuations (particularly Figure AI at $39.0B) and the extent to which Magic AI's technology moat and unit economics converge toward median-tier versus top-tier peer profiles.
